content:
For years, she visited my dreams—an impossible friend whose presence felt real enough to matter. We talked, laughed, sometimes argued, but always she was there, a constant in the chaos of youth. I never questioned it; it was just a strange quirk of my mind.

Years later, walking through a crowded street, I saw her. The same face, the same impossible familiarity. She recognized me, but not with warmth—rather, a sharp, almost fearful glance before she turned away, disappearing into the crowd.

I wanted answers. How could she be real? Was I losing my mind? Or had those dreams been memories of someone real, buried and forgotten? She avoided me, as if my questions were dangerous.

Maybe some things are just coincidences, or the brain’s way of stitching fragments into stories. But her look told me something different—something I couldn’t easily explain or dismiss.
